It is a member of the genus Schilleria, which is a group of plants commonly found in tropical regions. Schilleria heterophylla is native to the tropical regions of the Pacific Islands, where it grows in a variety of habitats. The classification of Schilleria heterophylla reflects its unique characteristics and evolutionary history within the Sapindaceae family.
